title: Deal
description: Central deal entity that ties together participants, assets, milestones, and documents across asset classes. A single deal can span multiple asset classes — e.g., an LBO with both credit facilities and equity co-investments.

DealParticipantAllocation.description: A single allocation of capital from a deal participant to a specific DealAsset. Represents the participant's committed amount to one component of the deal. The sum of a participant's allocations across all DealAssets equals their total deal commitment. Example: {"deal_asset_id": "da112233-...", "amount": {"amount": "300000000", "currency": "USD"}}

deal_asset_id.description: UUID reference to the DealAsset this allocation targets. Must reference a DealAsset that is part of the same deal. Example: 'da112233-4455-6677-8899-aabbccdde001'

amount.description: Capital amount this participant has committed to the specified DealAsset. A participant may allocate different amounts to different assets within the same deal. Always populated. Example: {"amount": "300000000", "currency": "USD"}

DealParticipant.description: A party participating in a deal, with their role, investment vehicle, target commitment, and per-asset capital allocations. A single party may appear multiple times if they participate through different funds or in different capacities. Early in the deal lifecycle, only target_commitment may be known; allocations are populated as the deal matures and asset-level breakdowns are finalized. Example: {"party_id": "...", "role": "LEAD", "fund_id": "...", "target_commitment": {"amount": "500000000", "currency": "USD"}, "allocations": [{"deal_asset_id": "da112233-...", "amount": {"amount": "300000000", "currency": "USD"}}]}

party_id.description: UUID of the Party entity representing this participant. Must reference an existing Party record. Example: 'a1b2c3d4-e5f6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890'

role.description: The participant's role in this deal (LEAD, CO_INVESTOR, ADVISOR, etc.). Determines governance rights and reporting obligations. Example: 'LEAD'

fund_id.description: UUID of the Fund entity through which this party is investing, if applicable. Omit for advisors, arrangers, and other non-investing participants. Links the deal participation to a specific fund vehicle for allocation and reporting purposes. Example: 'f1a2b3c4-d5e6-7890-abcd-ef1234560001'

target_commitment.description: The participant's desired total capital commitment to the deal. This is an indicative figure set early in the deal lifecycle, before asset-level allocations are finalized. In oversubscribed deals, the actual allocated total (sum of allocations) may be less than the target. Useful for pipeline reporting, capacity planning, and tracking demand vs. available deal size. Omit for non-investing participants (advisors, agents). Example: {"amount": "500000000", "currency": "USD"}

allocations.description: Per-asset capital allocations for this participant, populated as the deal matures. Each entry specifies how much capital the participant commits to a specific DealAsset. The sum of all allocation amounts represents the participant's actual committed capital — which may differ from target_commitment in oversubscribed or restructured deals. Omit for non-investing participants or when allocations have not yet been determined. Example: [{"deal_asset_id": "da112233-...", "amount": {"amount": "300000000", "currency": "USD"}}]

DealMilestone.description: A milestone marking a stage in the deal lifecycle. Milestones are additive — as the deal progresses, new milestones are appended. The most recent completed milestone indicates the current deal state. Example: {"stage": "IC_REVIEW", "date": "2024-04-01", "completed": true, "notes": "IC approved unanimously"}

stage.description: The deal stage this milestone represents. Example: 'IC_REVIEW'

date.description: Date this milestone was reached or is expected to be reached. For future milestones, this is the target date. Example: '2024-04-01'

completed.description: Whether this milestone has been completed. false = planned/in-progress, true = done. Future milestones should be false. Example: true

notes.description: Free-text notes on this milestone — IC decision rationale, conditions, blockers, or key outcomes. Example: 'IC approved unanimously, subject to final DD on environmental liabilities'

allOf.description: A deal is the central organizing entity that connects participants, assets, and milestones across one or more asset classes. A deal does NOT have a single asset class — each DealAsset carries its own asset_class, because a single deal can comprise credit, equity, real estate, and other components simultaneously.

name.description: Short, recognizable deal name used in internal communications and reporting. Typically includes the target company and deal type. Example: 'Acme Corp LBO'

description.description: Longer narrative description of the deal — the investment thesis, transaction structure, or strategic rationale. Useful for deal screening and IC presentations. Example: 'Leveraged buyout of Acme Corp, a leading industrial automation company with $200M EBITDA'

stage.description: Current stage of the deal in its lifecycle. Should correspond to the most recent completed milestone. Updated as the deal progresses. Example: 'ACTIVE'

deal_size.description: Total enterprise value or aggregate transaction size across all components. This is the headline number for the deal, not any single participant's commitment. Example: {"amount": "750000000", "currency": "USD"}

issuer_id.description: UUID reference to the Issuer entity — the company, government, SPV, or fund on the other side of this deal. The Issuer carries sector, jurisdiction, and classification attributes. Every deal should have an issuer; omit only for deals in very early sourcing where the target has not yet been formally created as an Issuer record. Example: 'is112233-4455-6677-8899-aabbccdde001'

sourced_date.description: Date the deal was first sourced or entered the pipeline. Used for tracking deal velocity (days from sourcing to close). Example: '2024-01-10'

expected_close_date.description: Target date for deal closing. May be updated as negotiations progress. Null or absent for passed deals. Example: '2024-06-01'

actual_close_date.description: Actual date the deal closed and funded. Only set once the deal has reached CLOSING/ACTIVE stage. Null for deals still in progress or passed. Example: '2024-06-15'

participants.description: All parties involved in the deal — investors, advisors, arrangers, agents. Each entry specifies the party's role, fund vehicle (if investing), and economic terms.

assets.description: UUID references to DealAsset entities that make up this deal. DealAssets are independent, persistent entities — each has its own identity, asset_class, and reference to a domain entity (Facility, Investment, Property, or LPInterest). A deal typically has one or more assets — e.g., an LBO might have a senior term loan, a mezz tranche, and an equity co-invest as separate DealAsset records. Example: ['da112233-4455-6677-8899-aabbccdde001', 'da112233-4455-6677-8899-aabbccdde002']

milestones.description: Ordered list of deal lifecycle milestones. Append new milestones as the deal progresses. The most recent completed milestone should match the deal's stage field.

documents.description: UUID references to Document entities associated with this deal — IC memos, term sheets, credit agreements, purchase agreements, etc. Example: ['dc112233-4455-6677-8899-aabbccdde001']
